activities.description: Arrive at O'Hare, take the Blue Line CTA train to The Loop (approximately 45 minutes), check into hotel
activities.notes: CTA train fare from O'Hare to downtown

activities.description: Visit the iconic 'Bean' sculpture and explore Millennium Park
activities.notes: Free attraction, walking distance from hotel

activities.activity: Deep-Dish Pizza Dinner at Lou Malnati's
activities.description: Enjoy authentic Chicago deep-dish pizza at this legendary establishment. Lou Malnati's offers kosher-style options and can accommodate dietary needs with advance notice.
activities.notes: Call ahead to discuss kosher dietary requirements. Classic deep-dish experience.

activities.description: Experience breathtaking views from the 103rd floor Skydeck, including The Ledge glass boxes extending 4.3 feet outside the building
activities.notes: Book tickets online in advance for best rates. Allow 1.5-2 hours for visit.

activities.description: Kosher-style deli with extensive menu options in the South Loop
activities.notes: Kosher-style establishment with traditional Jewish deli fare

activities.description: Explore one of the world's greatest art museums, featuring works from ancient to contemporary
activities.notes: Allow 2-3 hours. Home to Grant Wood's 'American Gothic' and Seurat's 'A Sunday Afternoon'

activities.activity: Group Dinner at David Burke's Primehouse
activities.description: Upscale steakhouse in the James Hotel offering premium cuts and elegant atmosphere for group dining
activities.notes: Reservations required. Can accommodate kosher dietary needs with advance notice. Known for dry-aged steaks.

activities.description: 90-minute guided boat tour showcasing Chicago's world-famous architecture along the Chicago River
activities.notes: Chicago Architecture Foundation tour. Dress warmly for October weather.

activities.description: Kosher-certified cafe offering bagels, sandwiches, and salads
activities.notes: Certified kosher establishment. Requires travel to West Rogers Park neighborhood.

activities.description: Explore Chicago's iconic lakefront destination with shops, attractions, and scenic views of Lake Michigan
activities.notes: Free to walk around; budget includes optional attractions or snacks

activities.activity: Farewell Dinner at Gibsons Bar & Steakhouse
activities.description: Classic Chicago steakhouse experience with generous portions and lively atmosphere
activities.notes: Iconic Chicago steakhouse. Call ahead regarding kosher dietary accommodations.

activities.description: Check out of hotel, take Blue Line CTA to O'Hare for 6:15 PM flight
activities.notes: Allow 2.5-3 hours before flight for travel and security

unmet_constraints: Kosher dietary needs may be challenging - while kosher-style options exist, fully kosher-certified restaurants are limited in the Loop area. The Bagelers Coffeehouse is certified kosher but requires travel outside downtown. Deep-dish pizza places and steakhouses may require advance coordination for strict kosher observance.
input.reasoning:
I've created a 3-day Chicago itinerary (October 20-23, 2026) for 3 travelers from New York with a hotel budget of $300/night near the Loop. The plan includes both requested attractions: Willis Tower Skydeck on Day 2 morning and deep-dish pizza at Lou Malnati's on Day 1 evening. I've incorporated two steakhouse group dinners (David Burke's Primehouse and Gibsons) as requested. 

For kosher dietary needs, I've included kosher-style options and one certified kosher establishment (The Bagelers), though this requires noting that strict kosher observance in Chicago's Loop area can be challenging. Most upscale restaurants can accommodate with advance notice.

The hotel (Hyatt Centric) is well-located in the Loop at $280/night (under the $300 budget), requiring 2 rooms for 3 travelers. Flights are realistic morning departure and evening return options. Activities balance the requested interests with classic Chicago experiences like the Architecture River Cruise, Art Institute, and Navy Pier.

Total cost is $3,965 for 3 people ($1,322 per person), covering flights ($1,140), hotel ($1,680), activities and meals ($1,145).
input.final_message_to_user:
I've created your 3-day Chicago itinerary (October 20-23, 2026) for 3 travelers! 

**Highlights:**
✈️ Round-trip flights from NYC to Chicago: $1,140 total
🏨 Hyatt Centric The Loop (3 nights, 2 rooms): $1,680 total
🌆 Willis Tower Skydeck with stunning views from the 103rd floor
🍕 Deep-dish pizza at legendary Lou Malnati's
🥩 Two group steakhouse dinners (David Burke's Primehouse & Gibsons)
🏛️ Art Institute, Architecture River Cruise, Navy Pier & more

**Total Cost: $3,965** ($1,322 per person)

**Important Note on Kosher Dining:** Chicago's Loop area has limited certified kosher restaurants. I've included kosher-style options and one certified kosher cafe (The Bagelers). For the steakhouses and Lou Malnati's, I recommend calling ahead to discuss your specific kosher requirements - many upscale establishments can accommodate with advance notice. If strict kosher certification is essential, you may want to bring some prepared foods or plan additional travel to West Rogers Park where more certified options exist.

Your hotel is perfectly located for walking to most attractions, and you'll experience the best of Chicago's architecture, food, and culture!
